producer: demiurge RTSC §8.6 — hexa-rtsc verifier output → conductor handoff (thin adapter, M465). McMillan / Allen-Dynes Newton inversion. Forward Tc(λ;ω_log,μ*), inverse λ(Tc). 5 SC anchors (Pb/Nb/V3Si/Nb3Sn/MgB2). BCS-λ ceiling reproduced numerically.

verifier.verifier_tier: T2 numerical (Newton solver leg of T2 ×3 stack)

spec.family_enum_note: ConductorMaterial = cu | nbti | nb3sn | rebco | bi2212 | rtsc_hypothetical (RTSC.md §5 Axis B / §8.6). Adapter records use rtsc_hypothetical for simulation-derived solver baselines — verifier output is computational lineage, not material characterization.
spec.compound_note: Simulation-derived solver baselines do not bind to a specific compound. The mcmillan solver exercises the Allen-Dynes 1975 Tc formula on five published reference SCs (Pb, Nb, V3Si, Nb3Sn, MgB2 σ-band) at runtime; the BCS-λ ceiling check (max Tc < 50 K at Θ_D=400 K, μ*=0.13, λ ≤ 1.5) is the F-RTSC-2 falsifier core. For per-material records, ingest a vendor-datasheet or external-paper record alongside this baseline.

jc_b_t_theta.description: Solver baseline records do not populate Jc(B,T,θ); that table comes from a vendor-datasheet or measurement-ingest record. Left null — see PR #90 sibling for the REBCO baseline schema.

provenance.source_citation: McMillan / Allen-Dynes Newton inversion. Forward Tc(λ;ω_log,μ*), inverse λ(Tc). 5 SC anchors (Pb/Nb/V3Si/Nb3Sn/MgB2). BCS-λ ceiling reproduced numerically. Verifier: stdlib/rtsc/verify/numerics_mcmillan_solver.hexa — invocation `hexa run stdlib/rtsc/verify/numerics_mcmillan_solver.hexa`. Sentinel `__HEXA_RTSC_NUMERICS_MCMILLAN_SOLVER__ PASS` on success.
provenance.derive_chain: hexa-rtsc stdlib/rtsc/verify/numerics_mcmillan_solver.hexa -> RTSC/conductor/hexa_rtsc_baseline_adapter.hexa -> exports/conductor/hexa-rtsc-baseline/mcmillan/<stamp>/conductor_mcmillan.json -> (downstream) device producer reads nu[Coil]/Jc_limit/Hc2_limit when family is bound

gate_type_enum_note: gate_type in { vendor-datasheet | hexa-rtsc-derived | external-paper } per RTSC.md §8.5. Solver baselines are hexa-rtsc-derived — absorbed stays false permanently per the derived/sim contract.
scope_caveats: hexa-rtsc verifier output ingested · sim-derived · not wet-lab
Solver baseline — encodes verifier IDENTITY (pillar=rtsc, falsifier=F-RTSC-2, sentinel) not a new numerical result. To re-run: hexa run stdlib/rtsc/verify/numerics_mcmillan_solver.hexa
absorbed=false permanent until an independent measurement record supersedes (RTSC.md §8.7 Tier-3).

family=rtsc_hypothetical — record is universal-form lineage (Allen-Dynes Tc formula), not a per-material characterization. For per-material binding (e.g., Nb3Sn-specific λ inversion), ingest a vendor-datasheet or measurement record alongside.
Sibling REBCO baseline at exports/conductor/hts_rebco/20260525T000000Z/conductor_rebco_hts_baseline.json (PR #90, also hexa-rtsc-derived, family=rebco).
r4_invariant: absorbed=false · provisional=true · gate_type=hexa-rtsc-derived · domain=conductor. R4 preserved — solver baseline is computational lineage, not measurement.
